ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of The Final Account By Theresa Griffith
Off Calendar Jul-25-2011 Continued To Aug-29-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for Successor Administrator. Theresa Griffith in Propria Persona.

Mr. Ferdon stated that Ms. Griffith has moved, that the mail they sent her has not been returned and that Ms. Lane (Paralegal) has not been able to get together with Ms. Griffith. Ms. Griffith stated that she can set up an appointment with Ms. Lane within one week. Ms. Griffith provided a mailing address and phone numbers to Mr. Ferdon in open Court. Status of Ms. Griffith's final account was continued to August 29, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of The Final Account By Theresa Griffith
Off Calendar Jun-20-2011 Continued To Jul-25-11 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for Successor Administrator.

The Court noted that the Former Administrator Theresa Griffith was not present. Mr. Ferdon stated that he offered assistance at the last hearing, that he gave Ms. Griffith's phone number to one of the Paralegals but that the Paralegal was unable to reach her, requested a continuance and stated that he will contact Ms. Griffith in writing. The matter was continued to July 25, 2011 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of The Final Account By Theresa Griffith
Off Calendar May-23-2011 Continued To Jun-20-11 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Theresa Griffith in Propria Persona. Jonathan Ferdon for Successor Administrator.

Ms. Griffith stated that she does not have a final account. Mr. Ferdon suggested that Ms. Griffith's two Daughters assist her with this matter, that Ms Griffith obtain a declaration from her Son explaining that the funds in the Grandmother's account belong to him and to include in the accounting that funds in the other account were hers from an award given to her involving an automobile accident. The matter was continued to June 20, 2011 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of The Final Account By Theresa Griffith

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of Proof/The Receipt For The Blocked Account
Off Calendar Mar-07-2011 Text Ruling

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-07-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Theresa Griffith (Administrator) in propria persona. Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator (SFPA).

The Court stated that there are pending issues and that there is the possibility of Ms. Griffith being removed as Administrator and the SFPA being appointed as Successor Administrator. In addition, the Court stated that it had reviewed the report filed by Mr. Ferdon. Mr. Ferdon stated that their report set everything out, that the Neighborhood Assistance Corporation of America (NACA) had been involved, that there was a hold on the property, that the hold was lifted, that there were other attorney's involved, that Ms. Griffith is now involved with Legal Assistance for the Elderly, that she is looking into predatory lending, and that she is also looking into why NACA lifted the hold. Ms. Griffith presented her concerns/issues as set forth on the record and stated that she did not have the funds to retain another attorney (Mr. Miyake). At the conclusion of argument/discussions, the Court removed Theresa Griffith as Administrator stating that she failed to comply with the Court's order to place the funds in a blocked account, and then appointed the San Francisco Public Administrator as the Successor Administrator of the Estate of Cecelia C. Wade. Court to prepare the order.

In addition, the Court stated that the first thing that the SFPA needs to do is to close the Estate, that the SFPA could file a petition for instructions if needed, and that it was setting May 2, 2011 as the status date for the filing of Theresa Griffith's final account. The Court advised Ms. Griffith to consult with an attorney if she did not know how to proceed.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Mar-07-2011 Text Ruling

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Feb-07-2011 Continued To Mar-07-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of Proof/The Receipt For The Blocked Account
Off Calendar Feb-07-2011 Continued To Mar-07-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-07-2011 Continued To Mar-07-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for Public Administrator. Theresa Griffith (Administrator) in Propria Persona. Leslie Griffith (Daughter) in Propria Persona. Angela Griffith (Daughter) in Propria Persona.

Theresa Griffith stated that she does not have proof of the blocked account, that she was reluctant to do something that she did not understand, that she could not afford an Attorney and requested the Public Administrator be appointed. Mr. Ferdon stated that he had little information regarding the case, that he had concerns with the status of the property and that there was not much the Public Administrator could do if the house was in foreclosure. The Parties met outside the courtroom to talk about the matter. Upon returning, Mr. Ferdon asked the Court not to appoint the Public Administrator at this point, that Angela volunteered to look into the matter, that the property was in foreclosure with a hearing date of February 18, 2011 and requested a one month continuance. The matter was continued to March 7, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Ferdon is to file a declaration by March 2, 2011 and apprise the Court of all information obtained.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-10-2011 Continued To Feb-07-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Theresa Griffith in Propria Persona.

Ms. Griffith stated that funds were not placed in a blocked account, that Ms. Lofdahl has the statements and that she did not receive everything back from Ms. Lofdahl. The Court stated that it would continue the matter for three weeks to give notice to the Public Administrator that the Court will remove Theresa Griffith and appoint the Public Administrator. Ms. Griffith stated that she has an appointment with Attorney Timothy Bereski on January 18th. The matter was continued to February 7, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of Proof/The Receipt For The Blocked Account
Off Calendar Jan-10-2011 Continued To Feb-07-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-20-2010 Continued To Jan-10-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Theresa Griffith in Propria Persona.

Ms. Griffith requested more time stating that she did not want to resign as Administrator, that she wanted to see things through, that she needed legal assistance and that she was in contact with a law firm. In addition, Ms. Griffith provided a copy of the Wells Fargo Bank statement dated August 10, 2010 and stated that the money was still in the Wells Fargo account. The matter was continued to January 10, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Griffith was directed to bring in original bank statements from the date she was appointed as Administrator to current (December 2010).

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Theresa Griffith in Propria Persona.

Ms. Griffith requested a continuance stating that she needed legal Counsel, provided the Court with the receipt showing that Ms. Lofdahl was paid, and stated that the money was in a Wells Fargo Bank account in her name. The Court stated that it wants to see the original bank statements and continued the matter to December 20, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. In addition, the Court stated that Ms. Griffith's proof of payment to Ms. Lofdahl provided in open court today would be returned by mail once it had been reviewed.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Theresa Griffith in Propria Persona.

Ms. Griffith stated that she will be filing the receipt for the blocked account, that the Bank needed the paper work with regard to the blocked account and that she is seeking the assistance of Counsel Linda Gilbert. In addition, Ms. Griffith explained why she did not appear at the last hearing and stated that she mailed a money order to Ms. Lofdahl on Friday. The Court stated that proof of the blocked account and proof of payment to Ms. Lofdahl was needed. The Court verified Ms. Griffith's address and stated that it would mail the document needed by the Bank to her address. The matter was continued to December 6, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of Proof/The Receipt For The Blocked Account
Off Calendar Aug-30-2010 Continued To Sep-13-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Aug-16-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Lofdahl for and with Theresa Griffith.

Ms. Griffith stated that she had no objection to the petition. Counsel and Ms. Griffith spoke of the difficulties they had in communicating with one another and the break down in the Attorney-Client relationship. Ms. Griffith provided copies of two checks, in the amount of $4,763.93 (auto insurance proceeds), one in the amount of $62.44 and a copy of a deposit receipt for the total for both checks. The Court noted that the account number on the deposit receipt did not match the Estate account. The Court informed Ms. Griffith that the funds needed to be deposited into an Estate blocked account. The blocked account is to be opened by August 25, 2010. Status of filing of proof/the receipt for the blocked account was set for August 30, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Griffith is to pay Ms. Lofdahl for the amount spent on publication. The Court granted the Motion to Be Relieved As Counsel (signing the order for the same in open court). Ms. Lofdahl is to serve notice of the motion and file a proof of service. Ms. Lofdahl is allowed to close the Estate account at Wells Fargo Bank.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Santiago.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Feb-11-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Lofdahl for and with Theresa Griffith. Leslie Griffith in Propria Persona.

Leslie Griffith expressed concern about Theresa's ability to sustain the house, stated that she was not hiring Counsel, and that she wanted the estate to be distributed equitably and closed out in the best possible way. Theresa Griffith stated that she would be fair to everyone, that she had a way and means to save the house and that she had support from some Family members. The Court granted the petition (subject to review of the order) without any powers under IAEA (Independent Administration of Estate Act) as publication was not proper. All estate assets are to be held in an Attorney-Client trust account maintained by Ms. Lofdahl.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Feb-02-2010 Continued To Feb-09-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jan-12-2010 Continued To Feb-02-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Lofdahl for and with Theresa Griffith. Leslie Griffith (Objector) in Propria Persona. Shannon Turner (Grandaughter) also present.

Leslie Griffith stated that she objected to Theresa Griffith being appointed, that the unsigned/unwitnessed will indicated that the Decedent wanted her to administer the estate and that she wanted to make sure that all Parties receive their share. In addition, she stated that there may be a conflict with Theresa and that she was willing to share the responsibilty of administering the estate. Ms. Lofdahl argued that there is no will, that her Client has just as much priority and that her Client took the initiative to file her own petition. In addition, Ms. Lofdahl stated that her Client took the necessary steps to prevent the property from going into foreclosure, that the estate asset would've been gone if it wasn't for her Client, that the estate would be supervised by the Court and asked for limited authority. The Court stated that the petition needed to be re-published and re-noticed, and suggested placing the funds into Ms. Lofdahl's Client Trust account. The matter was continued to February 2, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Turner suggested that the Parties share control and expressed the need for Leslie and Theresa to work together.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.
